title: React Router v7 routing setup
description: Set up React Router v7 in App.tsx with routes: / (SearchPage), /patients/:id (DashboardPage), /patients/:id/observations/new (ObservationFormPage), /cache (CacheStatsPage). All routes nested under AppLayout which uses Outlet for content rendering.

title: Step 3: FHIR Error Handling
description:
FHIR servers return OperationOutcome resources on errors. Map these to meaningful API responses instead of raw 500s.

Refs: docs/phase2.md lines 114-154, samfhir.md lines 619-621

Files to create/modify:
- backend/src/samfhir/domain/models/errors.py — domain error types
- backend/src/samfhir/adapters/outbound/hapi_fhir_client.py — catch and wrap FHIR errors
- backend/src/samfhir/main.py — register exception handlers

Domain error types:
- PatientNotFoundError(patient_id: str)
- FhirServerError(status_code: int, detail: str)

Exception handlers in main.py:
- PatientNotFoundError → 404 {error: patient_not_found, patient_id: ...}
- FhirServerError → 502 {error: fhir_server_error, detail: ...}
- ConnectionError → 503 {error: fhir_server_unavailable}

In the adapter, catch fhirpy exceptions and HAPI HTTP errors:
- 404 from HAPI → raise PatientNotFoundError
- 4xx/5xx from HAPI → raise FhirServerError with the OperationOutcome text
- Network failures → raise FhirServerError with connection context

Also update StubFhirClient to raise PatientNotFoundError instead of raw ValueError for consistency.
close_reason: Implemented domain error types, adapter error handling, and FastAPI exception handlers

title: API client layer (fetch wrapper + typed endpoints)
description: Build typed fetch wrapper in frontend/src/api/client.ts with ApiError class, BASE_URL from VITE_API_BASE_URL env var (defaults to empty string for same origin). Create frontend/src/api/endpoints.ts with one typed function per backend endpoint: searchPatients, getPatient, getPatientSummary, getConditions, getObservations, getMedications, getAllergies, createObservation, createCondition, getCacheStats, flushCache, getHealth.
close_reason: Created client.ts with ApiError class and typed fetch wrapper, and endpoints.ts with all 12 endpoint functions. TypeScript compiles clean.

title: TypeScript types mirroring backend DTOs
description: Define TypeScript interfaces in frontend/src/types/api.ts that mirror every backend Pydantic schema. Includes: PatientResponse, ConditionResponse, ObservationResponse, MedicationResponse, AllergyResponse, PatientSummaryResponse, CacheStatsResponse, HealthResponse, CreateObservationRequest, CreateConditionRequest, ApiErrorBody, ValidationErrorBody. Dates are ISO strings.
close_reason: Created frontend/src/types/api.ts with 12 TypeScript interfaces mirroring all backend Pydantic schemas

title: Patient dashboard page
description: Build DashboardPage.tsx using usePatientSummary(id). Display: PatientDemographics card (name, DOB, gender, ID), Add Observation button linking to form, tabbed clinical data via shadcn Tabs (Conditions, Observations, Medications, Allergies with counts). Create PatientDemographics.tsx and clinical table components: ConditionsTable, ObservationsTable, MedicationsTable, AllergiesTable. Use Badge for status fields. Handle 404 (patient not found) and 502/503 (FHIR server unavailable) errors.

title: TanStack Query setup and custom hooks
description: Configure QueryClient with staleTime 30s, retry 1, refetchOnWindowFocus false. Create custom hooks: use-patients.ts (useSearchPatients, usePatient, usePatientSummary), use-clinical.ts (useConditions, useObservations, useMedications, useAllergies), use-mutations.ts (useCreateObservation, useCreateCondition with query invalidation), use-cache.ts (useCacheStats, useFlushCache), use-health.ts (useHealthCheck with 30s polling). Use structured query keys.
close_reason: All 6 hook files created, tsc passes

title: Phase 2: HAPI FHIR Integration
description:
Replace the stub FHIR adapter with a live HAPI FHIR adapter. No auth required — HAPI's public R4 endpoint (http://hapi.fhir.org/baseR4) is open. Demonstrate reading from and writing to a real FHIR server.

See: docs/phase2.md (full implementation plan), samfhir.md §Phase 2 (acceptance criteria and decisions).

Acceptance Criteria (from docs/phase2.md lines 389-401):
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id} returns real patient data from HAPI FHIR
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id}/summary aggregates real data from multiple FHIR resources
- GET /api/v1/patients?name=... searches for patients by name
- POST /api/v1/observations creates an observation in HAPI
- POST /api/v1/conditions creates a condition in HAPI
- Cache stats show hits on repeated requests
- OperationOutcome errors returned as structured JSON
- Non-existent patient IDs return 404 (not 500)
- HAPI downtime results in 503 (not unhandled exception)
- make test passes (all existing + new tests)
- make lint passes with zero warnings
close_reason: All 9 sub-tasks completed — HAPI FHIR integration fully implemented with read/write operations, error handling, search, seed data, and tests.

title: Step 4: Write Operations — Domain Layer
description:
Add write capability to the port, service, and domain models.

Refs: docs/phase2.md lines 156-214, samfhir.md lines 613-616

Files to modify:
- backend/src/samfhir/domain/ports/fhir_port.py — add write methods
- backend/src/samfhir/domain/services/patient_service.py — add write methods
- backend/src/samfhir/domain/models/observation.py — add input models

New domain input models (in observation.py):
- CreateObservation: patient_id, code, display, value, unit (optional), effective_date (optional)
- CreateCondition: patient_id, code, display, clinical_status, onset_date (optional)

New FhirPort abstract methods:
- create_observation(observation: CreateObservation) -> Observation
- create_condition(condition: CreateCondition) -> Condition

New PatientService methods:
- create_observation — calls FhirPort.create_observation, invalidates patient cache
- create_condition — calls FhirPort.create_condition, invalidates patient cache

Note: after writing, invalidate the patient cache so subsequent reads reflect the new data.
close_reason: Added CreateObservation/CreateCondition domain models, FhirPort abstract methods, PatientService write methods with cache invalidation, and updated all adapters (MockFhirPort, StubFhirClient, HapiFhirClient stub). 2 new unit tests pass.

title: Step 1: HAPI FHIR Adapter — Read Operations
description:
Implement the core adapter that replaces StubFhirClient.

Refs: docs/phase2.md lines 32-87, samfhir.md lines 607-611

File to create: backend/src/samfhir/adapters/outbound/hapi_fhir_client.py

What it does:
- Implements FhirPort (all 6 existing abstract methods)
- Uses fhirpy.AsyncFHIRClient pointed at http://hapi.fhir.org/baseR4
- Maps FHIR R4 resources (fhir.resources Pydantic models) to our domain models (frozen dataclasses)

Method-by-method mapping:
- get_patient(id) → GET /Patient/{id} → Patient dataclass
- get_patient_summary(id) → Calls all search methods + get_patient → PatientSummary
- search_conditions(id) → GET /Condition?patient={id} → list[Condition]
- search_observations(id) → GET /Observation?patient={id} → list[Observation]
- search_medications(id) → GET /MedicationRequest?patient={id} → list[Medication]
- search_allergies(id) → GET /AllergyIntolerance?patient={id} → list[Allergy]

FHIR → Domain mapping details (docs/phase2.md lines 62-78):
- Patient.name[0].family → Patient.family_name
- Patient.name[0].given[0] → Patient.given_name
- Patient.birthDate → Patient.birth_date
- Condition.code.coding[0].code → Condition.code
- Condition.code.coding[0].display → Condition.display
- Condition.clinicalStatus.coding[0].code → Condition.clinical_status
- Observation.code.coding[0] → code/display
- Observation.valueQuantity.value + .unit → value/unit
- MedicationRequest.medicationCodeableConcept.coding[0] → code/display
- MedicationRequest.status → Medication.status
- AllergyIntolerance.code.coding[0] → code/display
- AllergyIntolerance.clinicalStatus.coding[0].code → clinical_status
- AllergyIntolerance.criticality → criticality

Handle missing/optional fields gracefully (HAPI data is inconsistent).

Constructor:
class HapiFhirClient(FhirPort):
    def __init__(self, base_url: str) -> None:
        self._client = AsyncFHIRClient(url=base_url)
close_reason: Implemented HapiFhirClient adapter with all 6 FhirPort methods, FHIR-to-domain mapping, graceful handling of missing fields, and 26 unit tests

title: Step 2: Wire HAPI Adapter into Application
description:
Swap the stub for the live adapter.

Refs: docs/phase2.md lines 89-113, samfhir.md lines 607-611

Files to modify:
- backend/src/samfhir/main.py — replace StubFhirClient() with HapiFhirClient(settings.fhir_base_url) in the lifespan function
- backend/src/samfhir/config.py — fhir_base_url already defaults to http://hapi.fhir.org/baseR4, no changes needed

Change in main.py:
  Before (Phase 1): app.state.fhir_client = StubFhirClient()
  After (Phase 2): app.state.fhir_client = HapiFhirClient(settings.fhir_base_url)

The import changes from stub_fhir_client to hapi_fhir_client. Everything else (dependency injection, routers, service layer) stays the same — that's the point of hexagonal architecture.
close_reason: Swapped StubFhirClient for HapiFhirClient in main.py lifespan. All 42 tests pass.

title: Add adapter-level unit tests for create_observation and create_condition
description: test_hapi_fhir_client.py covers _map_* functions and read operations but has no tests for the write methods. Add tests that mock self._client.resource(...).save() and verify: (1) the FHIR resource dict is built correctly from domain input, (2) ResourceNotFound raises PatientNotFoundError, (3) OperationOutcome raises FhirServerError, (4) aiohttp.ClientError raises ConnectionError. File: backend/tests/unit/adapters/test_hapi_fhir_client.py

title: Step 5: Write Operations — HAPI Adapter
description:
Implement the write methods in the HAPI adapter.

Refs: docs/phase2.md lines 216-247, samfhir.md lines 613-616

File to modify: backend/src/samfhir/adapters/outbound/hapi_fhir_client.py

create_observation implementation:
1. Build a fhir.resources.observation.Observation with:
   - status = 'final'
   - code.coding[0] from the input code/display
   - subject.reference = f'Patient/{patient_id}'
   - valueQuantity from input value/unit
   - effectiveDateTime from input effective_date
2. POST to /Observation via fhirpy
3. Map the server response (with assigned ID) back to our Observation domain model

create_condition implementation:
1. Build a fhir.resources.condition.Condition with:
   - clinicalStatus.coding[0].code from input
   - code.coding[0] from input code/display
   - subject.reference = f'Patient/{patient_id}'
   - onsetDateTime from input onset_date
2. POST to /Condition via fhirpy
3. Map the server response back to our Condition domain model

Also update StubFhirClient to implement the new write methods (append to in-memory lists and return with generated IDs).
close_reason: Implemented create_observation and create_condition in HapiFhirClient using fhirpy resource().save() API

title: Connection status indicator integration
description: Wire health check indicator into Header.tsx. Uses useHealthCheck() polling /health every 30s. Three states: connected (green), degraded (amber, API ok but Redis not connected), offline (red, health check failed). Shows pulsing gray dot on first load until first check completes. Does not block page rendering.

title: Add FHIR system URIs to coded values in write operations
description: create_observation and create_condition build coding entries without a 'system' URI. FHIR R4 recommends including system (e.g. http://loinc.org for observations, http://snomed.info/sct for conditions). Without it, HAPI accepts the resource but interoperability with other systems is degraded. Consider whether system should be part of the domain Create* models or hardcoded/configurable in the adapter. File: backend/src/samfhir/adapters/outbound/hapi_fhir_client.py

title: Propagate HTTP status code in FhirServerError from OperationOutcome
description: When catching OperationOutcome from fhirpy, we pass status_code=0 to FhirServerError, losing the actual HTTP status. Investigate whether fhirpy exposes the HTTP status code on the exception or response, and propagate it if available. Applies to all error handlers in HapiFhirClient. File: backend/src/samfhir/adapters/outbound/hapi_fhir_client.py

title: Inbound Adapters (API Routers)
description:
FastAPI routers and response schemas.

Files: backend/src/samfhir/adapters/inbound/__init__.py, inbound/api/__init__.py, inbound/api/health_router.py, inbound/api/patient_router.py, inbound/api/fhir_router.py, inbound/api/schemas/__init__.py, inbound/api/schemas/patient_schemas.py

Endpoints:
- GET /health — status + Redis connectivity
- GET /health/ready — readiness probe for Cloud Run
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id} — get patient
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id}/summary — aggregated summary
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id}/conditions
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id}/observations
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id}/medications
- GET /api/v1/patients/{id}/allergies
- GET /api/v1/cache/stats — hit/miss statistics
- DELETE /api/v1/cache — flush cache

Response schemas (patient_schemas.py): Pydantic DTOs separate from domain models.

Ref: docs/phase_1.md lines 203-237 (Step 4)
Ref: samfhir.md lines 383-401 (API Endpoints)

title: Outbound Adapters
description:
Implement outbound adapter implementations for FhirPort and CachePort.

Files: backend/src/samfhir/adapters/__init__.py, adapters/outbound/__init__.py, adapters/outbound/stub_fhir_client.py, adapters/outbound/redis_cache.py

stub_fhir_client.py: Hardcoded mock data for Jason Argonaut (Epic test patient). Full FhirPort implementation with no external calls. Swapped for epic_fhir_client.py in Phase 2.
redis_cache.py: CachePort via redis.asyncio — get/set(TTL)/delete/flush, stats (hit/miss via INCR), health check method.

Ref: docs/phase_1.md lines 180-201 (Step 3)
Ref: samfhir.md lines 339-371 (Redis Caching Strategy)
